LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS HYATT CORPORATION AND SUNSTONE EC5, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE CAUSE IS ARGUED AND SUBMITTED BY BOTH SIDES. THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING: OVERRULED. AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PREMISES LIABILITY, PLAINTIFF USES A JUDICIAL COUNCIL COMPLAINT TO PLEAD AROUND RECREATIONAL IMMUNITY, WHICH SOME DEFENDANTS APPARENTLY REFUSE TO ABJURE; THIS IS APPROPRIATE. AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PRODUCTS LIABILITY, DEFENDANTS RAISE FACTUAL DISPUTES INAPPROPRIATE TO A DEMURRER. FOR EXAMPLE, THE COMPLAINT PLEADS DESIGN AND ASSEMBLY RELATED TO THE REVOLVING DOOR. IT IS PLAUSIBLE THAT THE MOVING DEFENDANTS PLAYED SUCH ROLES; DISCOVERY SHOULD TELL. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR #6576

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS HYATT CORPORATION AND SUNSTONE EC5, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE. THE CAUSE IS ARGUED AND SUBMITTED BY BOTH SIDES. THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. PLAINTIFF USES A JUDICIAL COUNCIL COMPLAINT TO PLEAD AROUND RECREATIONAL IMMUNITY, WHICH SOME DEFENDANTS APPARENTLY REFUSE TO ABJURE; THIS IS APPROPRIATE. PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARE ADEQUATELY PLED. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR #6576

DISCOVERY 302: DEFENDANTS ASSA ABLOY ENTRANCE SYSTEMS US INC., HYATT CORPORATION, AND SUNSTONE EC5 LLC'S MOTION TO DEEM AS ADMITTED DEFENDANTS REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS, SET ONE TO PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED; ACCORDINGLY, PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO FILE WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER WITHOUT OBJECTION CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR ADMISSION, SET ONE. IF NO SUCH RESPONSES ARE FILED WITHIN THE TEN DAY, ALL REQUESTS SHALL BE DEEMED ADMITTED AND PLAINTIFF SHALL BE BARRED FROM PRESENTING ANY EVIDENCE TO THE CONTRARY. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL IS DIRECTED TO SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM. THE [PROPOSED] ORDER SHALL BE EMAILED IN WORD (.DOC) FORMAT DIRECTLY TO JUDGE PRO TEM PAUL RENNE AT RENNEPAR85@GMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: PAUL A. RENNE; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; NOT REPORTED. (D302)
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-01-2017 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-09-2017 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Nov-01-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ASSA ABLOY ENTRANCE SYSTEMS US INC., HYATT CORPORATION, SUNSTONE EC5, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues is Off calendar. Case settled. Dismissal of entire action filed October 19, 2017. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185, email:ADelgado@sftc.org, Reported. (D302)

MOTION To Deem As Admitted Defendants Requests For Admissions, Set One To Plaintiff
Off Calendar Oct-23-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS ASSA ABLOY ENTRANCE SYSTEMS US INC., HYATT CORPORATION, and SUNSTONE EC5 LLC'S MOTION To Deem As Admitted Defendants Requests For Admissions, Set One To Plaintiff is granted. No opposition has been filed; accordingly, plaintiff is ordered to file within ten (10) days from the date of this order without objection code compliant responses to defendant's request for admission, set one. If no such responses are filed within the ten day, all requests shall be deemed admitted and plaintiff shall be barred from presenting any evidence to the contrary. Defendant's counsel is directed to submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim. The [proposed] order shall be emailed in Word (.doc) format directly to Judge Pro Tem Paul Renne at rennepar85@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul A. Renne; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-12-2016 Set For Trial On May-30-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion To Strike And Motion To Strike
Off Calendar Aug-08-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Richard L. Frischer of Lamb & Frischer Law Firm LLP (415-997-9529) pappears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Katelyn M. Knight of Murchison & Cumming LLP (415-524-4300) appears as counsel for defendant/Demurring party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS HYATT CORPORATION AND SUNSTONE EC5, LLC'S Motion To Strike is DENIED. Plaintiff uses a Judicial Council complaint to plead around recreational immunity, which some defendants apparently refuse to abjure; this is appropriate. Punitive damages are adequately pled. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-08-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Richard L. Frischer of Lamb & Frischer Law Firm LLP (415-997-9529) pappears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Katelyn M. Knight of Murchison & Cumming LLP (415-524-4300) appears as counsel for defendant/Demurring party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS HYATT CORPORATION AND SUNSTONE EC5, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. As to the second cause of action for premises liability, plaintiff uses a Judicial Council complaint to plead around recreational immunity, which some defendants apparently refuse to abjure; this is appropriate. As to the third cause of action for products liability, defendants raise factual disputes inappropriate to a demurrer. For example, the complaint pleads design and assembly related to the revolving door. It is plausible that the moving defendants played such roles; discovery should tell. Written order is signed and filed in open court.
